title: pedicularis refracta var. transmorrisonensis
intro: Pedicularis refracta var. transmorrisonensis, also known as Trans-Morrisonese Pedicularis, is a plant species in the balm family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Chinese and Korean traditional practices, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and promoting respiratory health. In traditional herbal preparations, Pedicularis refracta var. transmorrisonensis is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
